steps.title: Make the two-row table
steps.content: This is a perfect linear-regression shortcut. At birth, $x=0$ and the whale is $160$ cm long. One month later, the length is $160+4.8=164.8$ cm. Put those two points into Desmos: $(0,160)$ and $(1,164.8)$.

steps.title: Run the linear regression
steps.content: Use the regression row $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=4.8$ and $b=160$, so the model is $$y=4.8x+160.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The slope must be $4.8$ centimeters per month, and the starting value must be $160$ centimeters. Choice D is the only option with both: $y=4.8x+160$. Choice D is correct.
